prompt: Create a single-page cinematic character design board for an original fictional character named “{argument name=\"character name\" default=\"[CHARACTER NAME]\"}”. Style: premium film pre-production pitch board, photorealistic cinematic concept art, professional visual development presentation, high-end art department aesthetics. Composition: Avoid generic grids, symmetrical layouts, or rigid template-like formatting. The page should feel like a professional director’s pitch board rather than a standard character sheet. Use an asymmetric, cinematic, story-driven composition with varied panel sizes, organic spacing, selective overlap, layered grouping, and a curated editorial rhythm. Let the overall board feel designed, intentional, and visually dynamic — like a real film proposal page created by a director, production designer, or concept artist. Character: {argument name=\"character description\" default=\"[Insert character identity, world, age, role, personality, outfit, materials, mood, and setting.]\"} Include: - one dominant hero portrait as the emotional focal point - supporting full-body turnaround views - expressive head angle studies - wardrobe or costume breakdown - material and texture detail callouts - character overview text - short production notes - color palette swatches - mood references or environmental fragments Design language: Use readable English headings, concise labels, thin divider lines, occasional callout arrows or handwritten notes, and a refined cinematic presentation style. Do not force every section into equal rectangles. Keep the layout fluid, premium, and visually curated. Quality: Highly detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, consistent character identity across all panels, realistic costume materials, believable text structure, no logos, no watermark, no celebrity likeness.
